nodes.name: CalculateCompositeSQI_ZZ2018
nodes.description: Calculates a composite Signal Quality Index (SQI) for a signal, using multiple detectors and parameters. This likely serves as an orchestrator or a specific implementation from a paper.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: Primary physiological signal waveform.

inputs.name: detector_1
inputs.constraints: Array of beat detections from the first detector.

inputs.name: detector_2
inputs.constraints: Array of beat detections from the second detector.

inputs.name: fs
inputs.constraints: Sampling frequency of the signal.

inputs.name: search_window
inputs.constraints: Window size for searching or comparison.

inputs.name: nseg
inputs.constraints: Number of segments for spectral analysis.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.constraints: Operational mode for the calculation.

nodes.type_signature: (signal: NDArray, detector_1: NDArray, detector_2: NDArray, fs: float, search_window: int, nseg: int, mode: str) -> float
nodes.conceptual_summary: MultiObserver_TimeSeries_ConcordanceMetric. Computes a single scalar score representing the degree of agreement between two or more independent sets of event markers relative to a primary one-dimensional data series. The process involves spectral analysis on segments of the primary series and statistical comparison of the event marker locations within specified tolerance windows. This fuses multiple discrete observations into a unified confidence or quality metric for the underlying series.. Applications: Geophysics: Fusing outputs from different P-wave detection algorithms applied to a single seismogram to derive a unified earthquake event confidence score., Financial Analysis: Measuring the strength of a trading signal by calculating the consensus between multiple technical indicators (e.g., moving average crossover, RSI threshold) on a price time-series., Astronomy: Combining candidate transient event markers (e.g., supernovae, FRBs) from different automated detection pipelines analyzing the same celestial light curve to produce a single, high-confidence detection alert., Manufacturing IoT: Assessing the operational health of machinery by measuring the agreement between anomaly flags generated by independent vibration, thermal, and acoustic sensors.

nodes.name: ValidateDetectorInputs
nodes.description: Checks if the R-peak detector inputs are empty. If either is empty, the signal quality is immediately deemed unacceptable.

inputs.name: detector_1
inputs.constraints: Array of R-peak locations.

inputs.name: detector_2
inputs.constraints: Array of R-peak locations from a second detector.

outputs.name: is_valid
outputs.constraints: False if either detector input is empty, True otherwise.

nodes.name: CalculateBaseSQIs
nodes.description: Computes four fundamental Signal Quality Indices (qSQI, pSQI, kSQI, basSQI) using external specialized functions.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: Input ECG signal.

inputs.name: detector_1
inputs.constraints: R-peak locations from the first detector.

inputs.name: detector_2
inputs.constraints: R-peak locations from the second detector.

inputs.name: fs
inputs.constraints: Sampling frequency.

inputs.name: search_window
inputs.constraints: Search window in ms for bSQI.

inputs.name: nseg
inputs.constraints: Frequency axis resolution for fSQI.

nodes.type_signature: (signal: NDArray, detector_1: NDArray, detector_2: NDArray, fs: float, search_window: int, nseg: int) -> tuple[float, float, float, float]

nodes.name: ClassifySQIsHeuristically
nodes.description: Classifies each of the four base SQIs into discrete categories (0=unqualified, 1=suspicious, 2=optimal) based on a set of heuristic rules, some of which are adaptive to heart rate.

inputs.name: qsqi
inputs.constraints: Beat-matching quality index.

inputs.name: psqi
inputs.constraints: QRS power-spectrum quality index.

inputs.name: ksqi
inputs.constraints: Kurtosis quality index.

inputs.name: bassqi
inputs.constraints: Baseline wander quality index.

inputs.name: detector_1
inputs.constraints: R-peak locations for heart rate calculation.

inputs.name: fs
inputs.constraints: Sampling frequency.

outputs.name: class_matrix
outputs.constraints: An array of four integer class labels.

nodes.type_signature: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float, detector_1: NDArray, fs: float) -> NDArray

nodes.name: AggregateHeuristicClasses
nodes.description: Aggregates the discrete classifications of the SQIs by counting classes to determine the final signal quality label ('Unacceptable', 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable').

inputs.name: class_matrix
inputs.constraints: An array of four integer class labels.

nodes.name: CalculateFuzzyMemberships
nodes.description: Transforms each of the four raw SQI scores into a vector of three fuzzy membership grades corresponding to 'Excellent', 'Barely acceptable', and 'Unacceptable' quality levels.

inputs.name: qsqi
inputs.constraints: Beat-matching quality index.

inputs.name: psqi
inputs.constraints: QRS power-spectrum quality index.

inputs.name: ksqi
inputs.constraints: Kurtosis quality index.

inputs.name: bassqi
inputs.constraints: Baseline wander quality index.

outputs.name: evaluation_matrix_R
outputs.constraints: A 4x3 matrix where each row is the fuzzy membership vector for an SQI.

nodes.type_signature: (qsqi: float, psqi: float, ksqi: float, bassqi: float) -> NDArray

nodes.name: AggregateFuzzyMemberships
nodes.description: Combines the fuzzy membership vectors using a weighted-average evaluation matrix to compute a final score (V), which is then mapped to a quality label.

inputs.name: evaluation_matrix_R
inputs.constraints: A 4x3 matrix of fuzzy membership grades.

nodes.name: CalculateBeatAgreementSQI
nodes.description: Calculates a beat-based Signal Quality Index (bSQI) based on the agreement between two beat detectors.

inputs.name: detector_1
inputs.constraints: Array of beat detections from the first detector.

inputs.name: detector_2
inputs.constraints: Array of beat detections from the second detector.

inputs.name: fs
inputs.constraints: Sampling frequency of the signal.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.constraints: Operational mode for the calculation.

inputs.name: search_window
inputs.constraints: Window size for comparing detector outputs.

nodes.type_signature: (detector_1: NDArray, detector_2: NDArray, fs: float, mode: str, search_window: int) -> float
nodes.conceptual_summary: MeasurePointProcessAgreement. Computes a scalar concordance metric between two discrete, ordered event series (point processes). It quantifies similarity by pairing events that fall within a specified proximity tolerance of each other. The final score is an aggregation of these successful pairings, often normalized by the total number of events, to represent the overall degree of alignment.. Applications: Genomics: Quantifying the consistency between two different peak-calling algorithms in ChIP-seq data by measuring the co-location of identified protein-DNA binding sites., Astrophysics: Cross-validating pulsar signal detections by measuring the timing agreement of pulses recorded by two independent radio telescopes to filter out instrument-specific noise., Finance: Detecting latency or execution discrepancies in high-frequency trading by comparing trade execution timestamps from two different exchange data feeds., Seismology: Measuring the reliability of an earthquake detection system by calculating the agreement in P-wave arrival times registered by two separate sensor arrays.

nodes.name: ConvertWindowToSamples
nodes.description: Converts the search window from milliseconds to an integer number of samples based on the sampling frequency.

inputs.name: search_window_ms
inputs.constraints: Time in milliseconds.

inputs.name: sampling_rate_hz
inputs.constraints: Sampling rate in Hz.

outputs.name: search_window_samples
outputs.constraints: Window size in number of samples.

nodes.name: CountMutualDetections
nodes.description: Counts the number of beats in the first detector's output that have a corresponding beat in the second detector's output within the search window.

inputs.name: primary_detections
inputs.constraints: Array of beat locations from the first detector.

inputs.name: secondary_detections
inputs.constraints: Array of beat locations from the second detector.

inputs.name: search_window_samples
inputs.constraints: The number of samples around each primary beat to search for a secondary beat.

outputs.name: mutual_detection_count
outputs.constraints: The total number of beats found by both detectors within the search window.

nodes.type_signature: (primary_detections: NDArray, secondary_detections: NDArray, search_window_samples: int) -> int

nodes.name: CalculateAgreementScore
nodes.description: Calculates the final bSQI score based on the number of mutual detections and the total number of detections from each source, according to the specified mode.

inputs.name: mutual_detection_count
inputs.constraints: Number of agreed-upon detections.

inputs.name: detector_1_count
inputs.constraints: Total number of beats in the first detector's output.

inputs.name: detector_2_count
inputs.constraints: Total number of beats in the second detector's output.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.constraints: One of 'simple', 'matching', or 'n_double'.

outputs.name: bSQI_score
outputs.constraints: The calculated beat-based Signal Quality Index.

nodes.type_signature: (mutual_detection_count: int, detector_1_count: int, detector_2_count: int, mode: str) -> float

nodes.name: CalculateFrequencyPowerSQI
nodes.description: Calculates a frequency-based Signal Quality Index (fSQI) using the power spectrum of the ECG signal.

inputs.name: ecg_signal
inputs.constraints: The ECG signal waveform.

inputs.name: fs
inputs.constraints: Sampling frequency of the signal.

inputs.name: nseg
inputs.constraints: Number of segments for spectral analysis.

inputs.name: num_spectrum
inputs.constraints: Numerator of the spectral ratio.

inputs.name: dem_spectrum
inputs.constraints: Denominator of the spectral ratio.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.constraints: Operational mode for the calculation.

nodes.type_signature: (ecg_signal: NDArray, fs: float, nseg: int, num_spectrum: NDArray, dem_spectrum: NDArray, mode: str) -> float
nodes.conceptual_summary: SpectralPowerBandRatio. Calculates a scalar metric representing the ratio of integrated power between two specified frequency bands of a one-dimensional discrete signal. The input signal is transformed from the time/sequence domain to the frequency domain via spectral analysis. The total power is then summed within a 'numerator' band and a 'denominator' band, and the ratio of these two sums is returned.. Applications: In seismology, to calculate the ratio of high-frequency energy (indicative of certain seismic events) to low-frequency ambient noise from sensor data., In audio engineering, to quantify the signal-to-noise ratio by comparing power in the speech frequency band to the power in bands associated with background hum or hiss., In mechanical engineering, for predictive maintenance by analyzing accelerometer data from a machine to find the ratio of power in fault-indicating high frequencies versus normal operational vibration frequencies., In economics, to analyze a financial time-series by comparing the power of short-term, high-frequency volatility against the power of long-term, low-frequency trends.

nodes.name: compute_power_spectral_density
nodes.description: Computes the power spectral density of the input ECG signal using Welch's method, which provides the signal's power distribution over a range of frequencies.

inputs.name: ecg_signal
inputs.constraints: Must be a 1D array of numerical signal data.

inputs.name: fs
inputs.constraints: Positive value representing the sampling frequency in Hz.

inputs.name: nseg
inputs.constraints: Positive integer for the length of each segment in Welch's method.

outputs.name: power_density
outputs.constraints: Power spectral density of the signal.

nodes.type_signature: (ecg_signal: NDArray, fs: float, nseg: int) -> tuple[NDArray, NDArray]

nodes.name: integrate_power_in_band
nodes.description: Calculates the total power within a specified frequency band by performing a trapezoidal integration over the power spectral density.

inputs.name: frequencies
inputs.constraints: Array of sample frequencies, must be monotonic.

inputs.name: power_density
inputs.constraints: Power spectral density corresponding to the frequencies.

inputs.name: frequency_band
inputs.constraints: A list containing the start and end frequency of the band, e.g., [5, 20].

outputs.name: band_power
outputs.constraints: The total power integrated over the specified frequency band.

nodes.type_signature: (frequencies: NDArray, power_density: NDArray, frequency_band: list[float]) -> float

nodes.name: calculate_power_ratio
nodes.description: Calculates the final SQI score from the numerator and denominator power values, applying the specified adjustment mode.

inputs.name: numerator_power
inputs.constraints: Power calculated for the numerator's frequency band.

inputs.name: denominator_power
inputs.constraints: Power calculated for the denominator's frequency band. Must be non-zero.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.constraints: Must be 'simple' for a direct ratio or 'bas' for an inverted ratio (1 - ratio).

outputs.name: fSQI_score
outputs.constraints: The resulting frequency-based Signal Quality Index.

nodes.type_signature: (numerator_power: float, denominator_power: float, mode: str) -> float

nodes.name: CalculateKurtosisSQI
nodes.description: Calculates a Signal Quality Index (kSQI) based on the statistical kurtosis of the signal.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: The input signal waveform.

inputs.name: fisher
inputs.constraints: Flag to indicate if Fisher's definition of kurtosis is used.

nodes.conceptual_summary: MeasureDistributionTailedness. Computes the fourth standardized moment of a 1D numerical array. This scalar value quantifies the 'tailedness' of the distribution, measuring the propensity for values to be concentrated far from the mean (outliers) relative to a Gaussian distribution. A boolean flag selects between the raw moment and the excess moment (adjusted by a constant).. Applications: Quantitative Finance: Assessing the tail risk of an asset's return distribution. High kurtosis indicates a greater probability of extreme market movements than predicted by simpler models., Industrial Process Control: Monitoring sensor data for anomalies. A change in the kurtosis of pressure or temperature readings can signal a deviation from stable operation and predict system failures., Geophysics: Characterizing the impulsiveness of seismic signals. The kurtosis of ground acceleration time-series helps differentiate between background noise and sharp, high-impact tremor events., Neuroscience: Analyzing neuronal firing patterns. The kurtosis of inter-spike interval distributions can distinguish between regular, rhythmic firing and bursty, irregular activity.
